title: ctenopteris parvula
intro: Ctenopteris parvula, also known as New Zealand maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is traditionally used in New Zealand for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from the flavonoids and terpenoids present in its fronds. The medicinal properties of Ctenopteris parvula have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb for traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
